title: Salthouse Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including Flg Off. William George Corewyn
narrative: On the night of 15 January 1945, Lancaster LM720 of 61 Squadron departed RAF Skellingthorpe in Lincolnshire as part of a major bombing operation against the Leuna synthetic oil plant near Merseburg, one of Bomber Command's priority targets in the final months of the war. The oil campaign was proving devastatingly effective at crippling Germany's war machine, but the heavily-defended Merseburg complex remained one of the most dangerous targets in the Reich. On the return flight, the Lancaster's crew encountered deteriorating weather conditions over the Norfolk coast. Poor visibility and misunderstood wireless telegraphy messages conspired to lead the aircraft dangerously low over the coast near Salthouse. At 01:21 hours, Lancaster LM720 struck one of the 200-foot steel masts of the Type 54 Chain Home Extra Low radar station on Bard Hill, just south of Salthouse village. Six of the seven crew members were killed instantly. Flight Sergeant Boakes, although pulled from the wreckage alive and badly injured, died shortly afterwards in hospital. The twisted remains of the radar mast stood for many years as a silent memorial to the crew, with poppy wreaths fastened to the steel by local people. A more formal memorial was later placed inside St Nicholas' Church in the village.

crew.name: Edward James Boakes
crew.fate: Killed (died in hospital shortly after crash)

cause_of_loss: Aircraft flew into 200ft Type 54 CHEL (Chain Home Extra Low) radar mast on Bard Hill in poor visibility; misunderstood W/T messages may have contributed
operation_target: Bombing operation to Leuna/Merseburg synthetic oil plant
memorial_details: | Field | Detail | |-------|--------| | Type | Wall-hung paper scroll on black backboard inside St Nicholas' Church, Salthouse; also poppy wreaths on radar mast remains | | Dedicated | 1995 (church memorial, for the 50th anniversary) | | Organised by | Adrian S. Pye (private enthusiast) | | Condition | Church memorial unverified condition; radar mast remnants still present on hill | | Access | St Nicholas' Church, Salthouse, Norfolk; Bard Hill accessible on foot | | IWM Reference | WMR-56361 |

external_narratives.summary: Detailed aircrew memorial page for Fg Off. William G. Corewyn and the crew of Lancaster III LM720 QR:D. Took off at 16:30 from Skellingthorpe for Leuna, Germany, and struck the 200ft CHEL radar mast at Bard Hill at 01:21 on the return. The radar station operators were tracking E-boats when the collision occurred.
key_details: Radar operators were tracking E-boats at time of collision

external_narratives.summary: IWM War Memorials Register entry for the crew of Lancaster LM720 memorial at St Nicholas' Church, Salthouse. National memorial register record with reference WMR-56361.
Photographic record of the memorial to Lancaster III LM720 at Bard Hill crash site. Shows the memorial marker placed at the location where the aircraft struck the radar mast.
key_details: Photographic documentation of crash site memorial

external_narratives.summary: Technical and historical details of the Bard Hill Chain Home Low radar station that the Lancaster struck. Documents the Type 54 CHEL installation and its role in coastal defence, providing context for why the 200ft masts were present.
key_details: Station was operational tracking enemy vessels at time of crash
